Title: Innovaccer <> Caduceus InterMountain RFP Discussion
Attendees: Baz Patel, Grant Knaggs, Jonathan Brown, Patrick Easterling
Opportunity Name: Caduceus Medical Group-Provider-Xccelerate Conference
Summary:
The meeting centered around the preparation and submission of a Request for Proposal (RFP) due on the fourth, with discussions on the necessary steps and resources required to meet the deadline.

Topics Discussed:
RFP Submission Timeline
The RFP was received on the morning of the meeting and is due on the fourth, with a target to submit responses by Monday to allow extra time for final adjustments.
Preauthorization Challenges
Preauthorization was identified as a weak area, with efforts being made to improve readiness.
Discussion on the need for manual intervention in complex cases and the importance of having staff to handle these situations.
RFP Document and Confidentiality
There is a formal RFP document, initially not intended for sharing, but necessary for the proposal process.
Innovate is already contracted, ensuring confidentiality.
Service Line and Volume Breakdown
The RFP includes a volume breakdown by service line, facility type, and region, covering inpatient and outpatient services.
Specific departments like Radiology, Cardiology, and General Surgery were highlighted.
Manual Process and Staffing
The need for manual processes in certain cases was discussed, with a proposal to create tiers based on complexity and time estimates.
The importance of having a skilled team to handle manual interventions was emphasized.
Enterprise Solution Scope
The RFP is for an enterprise solution across acute and ambulatory settings, with a focus on preauthorization for various departments.
Conclusion:

The meeting concluded with a plan to mobilize resources over the weekend to prepare a comprehensive response by Monday, ensuring all necessary details and estimates are included.

Attendees: Puneet Kochhar, David Friederichs, Brian Silverstein, MD, Galen Manning, Chelsea Kelley
Opportunity Name: Integris Health_AI COA, -Provider-Xccelerate Conference, Integris Health_eCQM
Summary:
The meeting primarily focused on data validation, file extraction, and resource allocation for ongoing projects.

Meeting Schedule
Discussion on the cancellation of the monthly call next week and the plan to consolidate updates in the March meeting due to scheduling challenges.
Data Validation and File Extraction
The team discussed the challenges in obtaining claims files and validating Epic data. Efforts are being made to work with Pam and Sarah for data validation between Health Catalyst and Innovator.
The importance of validating both internal Epic data and claims data was emphasized to ensure accurate quality measures.
Resource Allocation and Project Progress
Puneet Kochhar highlighted the need to utilize additional capacity to expedite contract onboarding and data extraction.
The team is working on obtaining data from independent practices and addressing barriers with THP practices.
There was a discussion on the need to work in parallel to gather data from various practices and payers.
File Management and Tracking
The team is using a tracker to monitor the status of file transfers and data extraction processes.
Challenges with missing files from payers like Blue Cross Blue Shield and Aetna were discussed, and strategies to retrieve or regenerate these files were considered.

The meeting concluded with an agreement to continue efforts in data validation and file extraction, with a focus on utilizing available resources to accelerate project timelines.

Transition Care Management (TCM) Use Case

Title: OH VBC <> Innovaccer Gravity team- Intro & Demo
Attendees: L898453M, Kaylee Adams, Maddie Schults, Sabrina Roude, Swapnil Bhagat, Sandeep Duvvuru, Smriti Kirubanandan
Opportunity Name: Orlando Health - VE Dashboard, Orlando Health_FFS Lives, Orlando Health-Provider-Xccelerate Conference, Orlando Health_Experience_Provider
Summary:
The meeting centered on the introduction and demonstration of the Gravity platform, a next-generation healthcare AI platform, and its potential applications for the participants' organization.

Topics Discussed:
Introduction to Gravity Platform
Overview of the Gravity platform as an upgraded version of the existing data activation platform.
Emphasis on its capabilities for enterprise-wide transformation, including AI governance, unified data, and self-service tools.
AI Agents and Use Cases
Demonstration of AI agents, particularly a chronic disease management agent, showcasing its ability to manage patient interactions and schedule appointments.
Discussion on the integration of AI agents with existing data systems like Epic and Data Bricks, highlighting the ease of data ingestion and quality assurance.
Data Integration and Quality
Explanation of the data studio's role in connecting to data sources, ensuring high-quality data for analytics and AI applications.
Discussion on the integration process with Epic and Data Bricks, including data normalization and governance.
Analytics and Visualization
Introduction to the Nova analytics engine, an upgraded version of the existing analytics tool, allowing for complex queries and visualizations using natural language.
Demonstration of the platform's ability to handle ad-hoc queries and provide insights through interactive dashboards.
Conclusion:

The meeting concluded with a discussion on potential use cases for the Gravity platform within the organization, focusing on diabetes management and other enterprise-wide applications. Participants were encouraged to identify high-priority use cases to explore further integration and implementation of the platform.

Title: Innovaccer/CC Soft Kickoff
Attendees: Grotheb, Beiene, Michael, Welchc2, Joe Thompson, Grossb, McLellan, Donald, Priyank, Medinam, Anand Dedhia
Summary:
The meeting was convened to discuss the pre-implementation phase of a project between Cleveland Clinic and Innovator, focusing on data integration and preparation before the formal contract is signed.

Topics Discussed:
Pre-Implementation Strategy
The meeting emphasized the importance of completing pre-work to ensure a smooth transition once the contract is signed. This includes data plumbing and engineering tasks to facilitate a quick go-live.
Introductions and Roles
Key personnel from Cleveland Clinic and Innovator were introduced, highlighting their roles in data and analytics, care management, and financial applications. Priyanka and Anand from Innovator will be primary contacts for implementation and delivery.
Data Integration and Management
Discussions centered around the methods of data transfer, such as SFTP and cloud sharing, and the need for a uniform data pipeline to reconcile patient lists. The importance of pre-implementation work to streamline data access and integration was highlighted.
Contract and Timeline Considerations
The execution of the contract is a key dependency for initiating the project timeline. The timeline may adjust based on the contract process, but the overall duration remains consistent.
Technical and Architectural Decisions
The meeting touched on potential architectural decisions, such as whether to send raw claims data to Innovator or pre-process it. The goal is to leverage Innovator's data models for efficiency.
Conclusion:

The meeting concluded with a focus on identifying stakeholders for future working sessions and the importance of pre-implementation work to ensure a successful project launch.

Title: Ellis<>Innovaccer: Call Center AI Tech Solution Proposal
Attendees: Wesley Sgroi, Bellowsm, Amink, Glenn Kunigan, Caitlin Spargo, pamela romano, Frank Fasano
Summary:
The meeting was centered around discussing a proposal for implementing AI solutions in a call center to improve efficiency and reduce workload.

Topics Discussed:

Wesley Sgroi introduced Frank Fasano, a VP on the product strategy team, to support the proposal discussion.
The team discussed the cold weather in Minnesota and exchanged pleasantries.
Proposal Overview
Wesley Sgroi outlined the proposal, focusing on AI tools for call centers, including AI copilot, scheduling agents, and support agents.
The proposal aims to streamline call center operations, reduce hiring needs, and improve ROI.
AI Copilot and Scheduling Agent
The AI copilot assists live agents by providing relevant caller information.
The scheduling agent manages patient calls for appointments, reducing agent workload.
Implementation and Integration
Discussion on integrating the solution with existing systems like Cerner and Sorian.
The team emphasized the need for a technical call to discuss integration specifics.
ROI and Pricing
The proposal includes a detailed ROI analysis, highlighting cost savings and efficiency gains.
Pricing is based on a transaction model, with different rates for scheduling and support agents.
Insurance Verification and Additional Features
The team discussed the potential for automating insurance verification during calls.
There was interest in exploring additional AI applications across other call centers within the organization.

The meeting concluded with plans for follow-up discussions on technical integration and insurance verification, as well as obtaining references and addressing contract terms.

Laura mentioned the use of a homegrown product, Inscript, and the challenges faced with their current partner, Sentrex, in managing 340b compliance and market access.

Attendees: Krum, Andrea, Kcoyle1, Eemiddleton, Sockloski, Amanda J., Along, Wasik, Scott M., Jantulov, Lady Hendley, Jennifer Lynn Ray, Kessler Brown, Andre Dias, Amy Stevens - Innovaccer, Patrick Leonpacher, Baz Patel, Prasath Venkatraman, Daren Barker
Summary:
The meeting was centered around a demonstration of Innovaccer's capabilities in automating prior authorization processes, with a focus on improving efficiency and reducing administrative burdens.

Topics Discussed:
Introduction and Team Setup
Amanda Sockloski and Baz Patel coordinated the meeting setup, confirming presenters and conducting sound checks.
Kessler Brown introduced the Innovaccer team and outlined the agenda, emphasizing the demonstration of their prior authorization solution.
Innovaccer's Capabilities

The platform's ability to automate processes and integrate with existing systems was emphasized, with a focus on reducing manual work and improving efficiency.
Demonstration of Prior Authorization Process
Jennifer Lynn Ray demonstrated the automated prior authorization process, showcasing how the system reduces the time from 28-40 minutes to about 3 minutes.
The system automates eligibility checks, medical necessity reviews, and documentation compilation, with options for manual review or automatic submission to payers.
Specialty Medication and Complex Cases
The demonstration included handling complex cases like chemotherapy, using NCCN guidelines to ensure compliance with medical necessity requirements.
The system's ability to handle additional documentation requests and peer-to-peer reviews was also discussed.
Integration and Customization
The platform's integration with existing EHR systems like Epic was discussed, ensuring that workflows remain unchanged while providing additional automation and insights.
The flexibility in configuring the system to meet specific organizational needs was highlighted.

Attendees: Maddie Schults (Innovaccer), Mtaha, Shossam, Ashish Mishra, Arezk, Mabdulhamied, Ahmed Mohamed Ragab, Muhammad Hasballah, Maliwaly, Sanjay J. (Innovaccer)
Summary:
The meeting was centered around gathering feedback on the Innovaccer Gravity platform, focusing on user experiences and areas for improvement.

Topics Discussed:
Introduction and Purpose
Maddie Schults introduced the session as a feedback gathering exercise to understand user experiences with the Gravity platform and identify areas for enhancement.
User Feedback on Platform Functionality
Mabdulhamied and other participants shared their experiences, highlighting the need for more user-friendly features, especially for non-technical users.
Concerns were raised about missing functionalities and the need for customization to cater to different user personas.

Samrat Mehta demonstrated how to create an opportunity on a test account, starting from the top of the funnel to contract closure.

Salesforce Functionality

Opportunity Name: Ascension Health_Post Acute Analytics, Ascension Health_Sara for Provider, Ascension Health_IntelliH, Ascension - Population Health Platform, Ascension Health_Quality Reporting (eCQM/GPRO) for TX Market, Network Health - Risk Adjustment, Ascension_Pop Health_Co-Pilot, Ascension_Pre Production Environment, Ascension-Provider-Xccelerate Conference, Ascension _Mini-Xccelerate Conference, Ascension - Performance Incentives at POC, Ascension_Productivity_Provider, Ascension_Productivity_Flow - Coding

Summary:
The meeting was centered around a demonstration by Innovator, the fourth and final vendor being considered for clinical quality reporting solutions. The session aimed to explore Innovator's capabilities in providing enterprise solutions for electronic clinical quality measures (eCQMs) and other quality reporting needs.

Topics Discussed:
Introduction and Vendor Background
Aeron Sheeley introduced the session, highlighting the presence of representatives from various sectors, including quality, safety analytics, and both ambulatory and acute care settings.
John Hickey from Innovator provided an overview of their existing infrastructure and partnership with Ascension.
Core Capabilities and Demonstration
Innovator's focus on solutions features, implementation, and reporting was discussed.
A demonstration of the base eCQM reporting tools was conducted, showcasing the ability to handle various reporting needs, including MEPS and Medicare reporting.
The flexibility of Innovator's tools to provide enterprise-level solutions and the ability to compare across markets were emphasized.
Data Integration and Reporting
Innovator's established connections with various electronic health records (EHRs) were highlighted, facilitating the integration of QRDA information.
The process of data ingestion, validation, and the ability to generate submission-ready files for CMS was explained.
The importance of data completeness and the ability to track performance throughout the year were discussed.
Implementation and Support
The implementation process for ACO reporting was outlined, including timelines and milestones for data integration and dashboard deployment.
Innovator's support structure, including training and tech support, was detailed, emphasizing existing processes with Ascension.
Conclusion:

The meeting concluded with a discussion on Innovator's readiness to support Ascension's quality reporting needs, highlighting their established processes and experience with various EHR systems.

The meeting centered around discussing data integration and predictive analytics for wound care management across multiple clinics.

Topics Discussed:
Data Integration Challenges
The primary challenge is extracting wound care data from hospital EHRs to use for various reporting and predictive modeling.
The current system involves manual transcription from hospital EHRs to their proprietary EHR, which is inefficient.
The team is exploring solutions to streamline data extraction and integration without compromising proprietary data.
Predictive Analytics and Clinical Decision Support
The goal is to provide clinicians with real-time insights and predictive models to enhance wound care decision-making.
Predictive models aim to alert providers about patients' healing trajectories and suggest management strategies.
The team discussed leveraging existing models developed in partnership with academic institutions to augment clinical intelligence.
Presentation and Usability of Data
There is a need for effective presentation of data to users, potentially through a side-by-side application with existing EHRs.
The focus is on creating a unified database and presenting predictive analytics in a user-friendly manner.
The discussion included the possibility of using AI tools to process and interpret clinical notes for better data extraction.

Metrics and Data Management
